new_classes.label_en: grape wine or grape marc (distillation feedstock)
new_classes.definition_en: A material entity consisting of grape wine or grape marc that serves as the feedstock for distillation to produce spirits classified under CN subheading 2208 20.

new_classes.label_en: grape wine or grape marc distillation process
new_classes.definition_en: A distillation process whose feedstock is grape wine or grape marc; includes primary distillation after alcoholic fermentation of grape wine (yielding wine distillate / raw distillate) as well as subsequent distillation steps. Produces spirits classified under CN subheading 2208 20.

new_classes.label_en: spirit obtained by distilling grape wine or grape marc (CN 2208 20)
new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous beverage of heading 2208 that is produced exclusively by distilling grape wine or grape marc, including wine distillate (raw distillate of wine) obtained by primary distillation after alcoholic fermentation of grape wine. It does not have the properties of neutral ethyl alcohol nor of a finished spirit liqueur.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the three main distinguishing criteria of CN 2208 20: (1) the product is a spirituous beverage under heading 2208, (2) produced by distillation, and (3) the distillation feedstock is grape wine or grape marc (including primary distillation / wine distillate). The GrapeDistillateFeedstock class covers both grape wine (linked via WineOfFreshGrapes) and grape marc as feedstocks, and the GrapeDistillationProcess subclass of DistillationProcess captures the production route. A minor coverage gap exists because (a) grape marc is not separately modelled as a named class in the TBox (it is described textually in the feedstock definition but not formally linked via a named class restriction, since no GrapeMarc class exists in the TBox), and (b) the negative criterion ('does not have the properties of neutral ethyl alcohol') is not formally expressed as a complement restriction, as adding such a restriction risks over-constraining the ontology without a neutral-ethyl-alcohol class in the current TBox. These gaps account for the ~18% coverage reduction.
